sections.paragraph:
& בַּעַד (comp. No. 2), with suff. בַּעֲדִי and בַּעֲדֵנִי Psa 139:11, בַּעֲדֹו, בַּעַדְךָ, in pause בַּעֲדֶךָ, בַּעֲדֹו, בַּעֲדֵנוּ, once בַּעֲדֵינוּ Amo 9:10, בַּעַרְכֶם, בַּעֲדָם pr. subst. but from the usage of the language, a prep. denoting any kind of nearness.-

(1) by, near; 1Sa 4:18, בְּעַד יַד הַשַּׁעַר “by the side of the gate,” and metaph. because of (comp. אֶל A, 7); Pro 6:26, בְּעַד אִשָּׁה זֹונָה עַד־כִּכַּר לָֽחֶם “because of a harlot (he comes) to a morsel of bread.”

(2) behind, after (Arab. بَعْدُ, بَعْدَ after, used of time). Gen 7:16, וַיִּסְגֹּר יְהֹוָה בַּעֲדֹו “and Jehovah shut up after him;” Jud. 3:22 Amo 9:10, לֹא תַגִּישׁ וְתַקְדִּים בַּעֲדֵינוּ הָרָעָה “evil will not come near us, and fall upon us behind us;” 2Ki 1:2 2Sa 20:21, בְּעַד הַחֹומָה prob. for מִבַּעַד הַחֹומָה “from behind the wall.” מִבַּעַד לְ i.q. בַּעַד (like מִתַּחַת לְ i.q. תַּחַת). Son 3:1, “thine eyes are like dove’s eyes מִבַּעַד לְצַמָּתֵךְ behind thy vail” (not, dazwifchen hervor, from behind, as in 2nd [Germ.] ed. and in Winer, which would be מִבַּעַד צ׳), verse , , .

(3) round about; Psal. 139:11 , וְלַיְלָה אֹור בַּעֲדֵנוּ “the night is light around me,” Job 1:10 3:23 Lam 3:7, גַּדַר בְּעֲדִי “he has obstructed the way around me” so that I cannot go out. In this signification it is joined-(a) with verbs of shutting, as סָגַר בְּעַד פ׳ to shut up any thing (pr. to shut around, einfchlieben), 1Sa 1:6 עָצַר בְּעַד Gen 20:18 חָתַם בְּעַד to shut with a seal, seal up, einfiegeln, Job 9:7 comp. Jon 2:6.-(b) with a verb of protecting, הֵגֵן בְּעַד pr. to fortify around any one, to surround with a bulwark, Psa 3:4 Zec 12:8.

(4) between (two things), into, among, zwifchen (etwas) hinein, Joe 2:8 durch (etwas) hin, as בְּעַד הַחַלֹּון with a verb of coming, Joe 2:9 looking out, Gen 26:8 Jud. 5:28 . Metaph.-

(5) pro, for (from the sense of exchanging, see Æhe.) 1Sa 7:9 2Sa 20:21, e.g. to supplicate (see הִתְפַּלֵּל), to make atonement (see בִּפֶּר), to consult an oracle ( Jer 21:2 Isa 8:19), to bribe a judge ( Job 6:22) for any one; Job 2:4, עֹור בְּעַד עֹור “skin for skin” (see עֹור ).
